title: Build Error on Windows with Clang: clang++: error: no such file or directory: '/bigobj'
body:
**Title:**  
Build Error on Windows with Clang: `clang++: error: no such file or directory: '/bigobj'`

**Description:**  
When building Verilator on Windows using Clang, an error occurs due to CMake configuration issues related to the `/bigobj` flag. The specific error message is:

```
[9/149] Building CXX object src/CMakeFiles/verilatorRelease.dir/V3Const__gen.cpp.obj
FAILED: src/CMakeFiles/verilatorRelease.dir/V3Const__gen.cpp.obj
C:\PROGRA~1\LLVM\bin\CLANG_~1.EXE -DYYDEBUG -DYY_NO_UNISTD_H -ID:/projects/tools/win_flex_bison-2.5.25 -ID:/projects/verilator/src/../include -ID:/projects/verilator/build/src -ID:/projects/verilator/build/src/../include -ID:/projects/verilator/src -ID:/projects/verilator/src/../platform/win32 -O3 -DNDEBUG -std=gnu++20 -Xclang -flto-visibility-public-std -D_MT -Xclang --dependent-lib=libcmt /bigobj -MD -MT src/CMakeFiles/verilatorRelease.dir/V3Const__gen.cpp.obj -MF src\CMakeFiles\verilatorRelease.dir\V3Const__gen.cpp.obj.d -o src/CMakeFiles/verilatorRelease.dir\V3Const__gen.cpp.obj -c D:/projects/verilator/build/src/V3Const__gen.cpp
clang++: error: no such file or directory: '/bigobj'
```

**Solution:**  
By modifying the CMake script around line 512, the issue can be resolved. The modification involves using the `-Wa,-mbig-obj` flag for Clang instead of `/bigobj`. Below is the adjusted CMake configuration:

```cmake
...
if (WIN32)
    if(MINGW)
        target_compile_options(${verilator} PRIVATE -Wa,-mbig-obj)
        target_link_options(${verilator} PRIVATE -Wl,--stack,10000000 -mconsole -lcomctl32 -DWIN_32_LEAN_AND_MEAN)
    elseif(CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "Clang")
        target_compile_options(${verilator} PRIVATE -Wa,-mbig-obj)
    else()  # MSVC
        target_compile_options(${verilator} PRIVATE /bigobj)
        target_link_options(${verilator} PRIVATE /STACK:10000000)
    endif()

    target_compile_definitions(${verilator} PRIVATE
        YY_NO_UNISTD_H
    )
    target_include_directories(${verilator} PRIVATE ../platform/win32)
    target_link_libraries(${verilator} PRIVATE bcrypt psapi)
endif()

install(TARGETS ${verilator})
```

**Explanation:**  
- For **MinGW**, the `-Wa,-mbig-obj` flag is used to allow large object files.
- For **Clang**, the same `-Wa,-mbig-obj` flag replaces the `/bigobj` flag, which Clang does not recognize, thus preventing the `no such file or directory: '/bigobj'` error.
- For **MSVC**, the original `/bigobj` flag is retained.

**Request:**  
Please consider updating the CMake configuration to handle the `/bigobj` flag appropriately when using Clang on Windows to prevent build errors.

**Additional Information:**  
- **Environment:** Windows10 22H2
- **Build Tool:** CMake 3.30.4
- **Compiler:** Clang 19.1.0
- **Affected File:** `CMakeLists.txt` around line 512
